Transfer of FunctionsUnited States Information Agency (other than Broadcasting Board of Governors and International Broadcasting Bureau) abolished and functions transferred to Secretary of State, see sections 6531 and 6532 of this title.

children.heading: AID-funded programs
children.label: a AID-funded programs
children.text: The Congress urges the administrator of the agency primarily responsible for administering part I of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 [ et seq.], in implementing programs authorized under that part, to increase assistance for undergraduate scholarships for students of limited financial means from developing countries to study in the United States at United States institutions of higher education. To the maximum extent practicable, such scholarship assistance shall be furnished in accordance with the guidelines contained in .22 U.S.C. 2151section 4704 of this title

children.heading: USIA-funded postgraduate study in the United States
children.label: b USIA-funded postgraduate study in the United States
children.text: The Congress urges the Director of the United States Information Agency to expand opportunities for students of limited financial means from developing countries to receive financial assistance for postgraduate study at United States institutions of higher education.

children.heading: Study by Americans in developing countries
children.label: c Study by Americans in developing countries
children.text: The Congress urges the President to take such steps as are necessary to expand the opportunities for Americans from all economic classes to study in developing countries.
